entities.name: jñāna
entities.cite: A cognition, on this view, is self-revealing

entities.name: jñeya
entities.cite: in knowing the pot, awareness displays the pot's form and its own

entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: The word, Bhartrhari says, has the same double luminosity

entities.name: artha
entities.cite: It presents artha-rupa, the meaning, and simultaneously sva-rupa, itself

axioms.name: sva-prakashate
axioms.cite: A cognition, on this view, is self-revealing

axioms.name: jneya-prakashate
axioms.cite: in knowing the pot, awareness displays the pot's form and its own

axioms.name: sva-prakashate
axioms.cite: It presents artha-rupa, the meaning, and simultaneously sva-rupa, itself

axioms.name: artha-prakashate
axioms.cite: It presents artha-rupa, the meaning, and simultaneously sva-rupa, itself

axioms.name: sadrsya
axioms.cite: The word, Bhartrhari says, has the same double luminosity

claims.name: jneya-prakashate
claims.cite: the form of the object known

rejected_readings.label: sabda_as_property
rejected_readings.rendering: The linguistic property of sound conveys meanings through its structured patterns.
rejected_readings.why: Demotes śabda from linguisticItem to property. Properties lack ontological independence and cannot prakashate (shine forth) or reveal themselves. This sort error makes the self-luminosity claim unlicensed and severs the analogy with cognition.

rejected_readings.label: jneya_as_absolute
rejected_readings.rendering: The object of cognition is an unchanging absolute that reveals itself through awareness.
rejected_readings.why: Promotes jñeya to absolute rather than manifestation. This contradicts the prakashate relation: absolutes are not manifested by other entities. The object must be a manifestation—something cognition reveals—not a co-equal absolute.

notes: The contract formalizes the double-luminosity doctrine (āvaraṇa-śakti) as applied to both cognition and word. Four predicates encode prakashate (self-revealing, other-revealing); one relation encodes the analogy (sadrsya). The framework assumes the Prabhakara Mimamsa model of self-luminous cognition and extends it to language, a signature Bhartṛhari move. No contradictions are explicitly ruled out in the commentary, hence denials = 0.
